description_long: This object is a vintage sewing machine with a cylindrical turquoise body mounted on a rectangular reddish-brown wooden base supported by four spherical feet. The machine features two prominent vertical posts at each end, with ornate knobs and a horizontal bar connecting them, likely for thread guidance. A metallic bowl-shaped component is attached to the top, connected by a wire, possibly for lubrication or thread feeding. The machine has several visible adjustment knobs and a handwheel, as well as a wire extending from the top, suggesting electrical operation. The overall design is mechanical and industrial, with a combination of metal and wood materials and a visibly aged patina.
description: A vintage sewing machine with a turquoise cylindrical body, mounted on a reddish-brown wooden base with ornate knobs, a handwheel, and a metallic bowl on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the turquoise cylinder end, two vertical posts with ornate knobs, and the metallic bowl centered at the top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full length of the turquoise cylinder, two large metal brackets, the handwheel, and the wire connecting to the bowl.
description_view_2: Rear view with the opposite end of the cylinder, two posts, and the bowl, mirroring the front but with less visible detail on the handwheel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cylinder, brackets, and more visible wiring and adjustment knobs, with a clearer view of the machine's labeling.
